scenarios.reasoning: The prediction gives about twice D and justifies it from Stage 2 rules 1, 2, and 4: fixed push and heaviness give fixed pace, so distance is proportional to time. No banned token or disqualifying pattern appears.
The prediction gives about twice T and justifies it from Stage 2 rule 3: falling has one fixed downward pace, so doubling height doubles time. No banned token or disqualifying pattern appears.

The prediction says the forward pace ends when the hand stops pushing and the ball falls straight down, landing directly below the release point. This matches the F=mv column and is derived from Stage 2 rules 1 and 3.

The prediction names B as the winner and gives the required 2:1 time ratio, derived from rule 2: same effort and twice the heaviness gives half the pace and twice the time. No banned token or disqualifying pattern appears.

The prediction says that when one push is removed, the block immediately takes the full steady pace produced by the remaining push, with no gradual build-up. This matches the F=mv column and follows from Stage 2 rules 1 and 2.
